info.name: AIR Intelligence System — Complete Test Suite
info.description:
Ready-to-run collection for all AIR endpoints using AIR_Log_Report_Merged.xlsx (10 real incidents).

▶ BEFORE YOU START:
1. Start server: uvicorn src.api.main:app --reload
2. Run 'S1-1 Ingest Excel' first
3. Copy any incident_id from that response → Edit Collection → Variables → paste into 'incident_id'
4. Run the rest in order

⚠ Qdrant is in-memory — data is lost on server restart. Always re-run S1-1 after a restart.

variable.description: After running S1-1, copy any incident_id from the response array and paste it here

item.name: STAGE 1 — Ingest + Status
item.description: Load all 10 incidents from Excel into Qdrant and verify the collection.

item.name: S1-1 Ingest Excel (file upload)

formdata.description: Select AIR_Log_Report_Merged.xlsx — the test script auto-saves the first incident_id to the collection variable

request.description:
Parse + embed + store all incidents from Excel in one call.

Expected response:
{
  "ingested": 10,
  "incident_ids": ["uuid1", "uuid2", ...],
  "collection": "incidents",
  "dimension": 1024
}

The test script automatically sets the {{incident_id}} collection variable to the first ID in the response — no manual copy needed.

item.name: S1-2 Check Collection Status
STAGE 2 — Semantic Search
item.description: POST /retrieval/search — all queries are pre-filled from the real incident data in AIR_Log_Report_Merged.xlsx

item.name: S2-1 Search: Difficult Intubation

request.description:
Matches against:
- Incident 1: 21-30 Male, Emergency Vascular/Ortho, CL IIb → BURP → bougie → ETT size 8
- Incident 5: 51-60 Male, Emergency Laparotomy, RSI + cricoid pressure, COPD + diabetes
- Incident 9: 41-50 Female, Bariatric Sleeve, BMI 43, anticipated difficult airway, video laryngoscopy

Expect: rank 1 score > 0.7

item.name: S2-2 Search: Bronchospasm + Equipment Failure

request.description:
Matches against:
- Incident 3: 21-30 Female, Gynecology DHC, bronchospasm + insufflator malfunction + gas supply problems, outcome E1
- Incident 4: 41-50 Female, Laparoscopic cholecystectomy, bronchospasm after peritoneal insufflation, desaturation, hypotension, outcome D2

item.name: S2-3 Search: Failed Spinal Anaesthesia

request.description:
Matches against:
- Incident 7: 31-40 Male, Orthopedic ACL reconstruction, spinal 0.5% heavy bupivacaine L3-L4, block height inadequate → converted to GA (ETT), outcome D1

item.name: S2-4 Search: Brainstem Anaesthesia After Regional Block

request.description:
Matches against:
- Incident 6: 61-70 Female, Ophthalmology cataract surgery, peribulbar block → 10 min post-block airway obstruction → brainstem anaesthesia → LMA rescue, bradycardia + hypotension, outcome D2

item.name: S2-5 Search: Paediatric IV Access Emergency

request.description:
Matches against:
- Incident 8: 0-10 Male, Emergency Paediatric inguinal hernia repair, sevoflurane induction → IV access failed → IO line placed + caudal block, outcome D1

item.name: S2-6 Search with Filter: General Surgery Only

request.description:
Filtered to General surgery. 3 matching incidents in dataset:
- Incident 4: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy (bronchospasm)
- Incident 5: Exploratory laparotomy (RSI difficult intubation)
- Incident 9: Bariatric sleeve gastrectomy (difficult airway)

Check: filters_applied=true, all results have surgery_type='General surgery'

item.name: S2-7 Search with Filter: Ophthalmology Only

request.description:
Filtered to Ophthalmology (1 matching incident):
- Incident 6: Cataract surgery, peribulbar block → brainstem anaesthesia

Expect: count=1, score will be the top match.

item.name: STAGE 3 — Similar Incident Lookup
item.description: POST /retrieval/search/similar — finds incidents closest to a stored one using its vector directly (no re-embedding). Requires {{incident_id}} to be set from Stage 1.

item.name: S3-1 Find Similar to Stored Incident
STAGE 4 — RAG Retrieval
item.description: POST /retrieval/rag — semantic search + formatted context block ready for LLM injection. The context_text field contains the narrative passages.

item.name: S4-1 RAG: Airway Obstruction After Regional Block

request.description:
Returns context_text formatted for LLM prompt injection.

Top match should be Incident 6 (61-70 Female, peribulbar → brainstem anaesthesia → LMA rescue → ventilatory support).

Check context_text: should contain incident narrative blocks with types, severity, and root cause info.

item.name: S4-2 RAG: Emergency Difficult Airway Management

request.description:
RAG with surgery_type filter.

Expect results from General surgery incidents:
- Incident 5: RSI + cricoid pressure, known COPD, post-induction hypotension in septic patient
- Incident 4: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy bronchospasm + hypotension
- Incident 9: Bariatric, video laryngoscopy

item.name: S4-3 RAG: Vasovagal + Cardiac Complications

request.description:
Matches:
- Incident 10: 71-80 Male, Emergency Cardiothoracic ICD insertion, traumatic pneumothorax, local anaesthesia + sedation → vasovagal response → atropine, outcome D2
- Incident 6: Peribulbar block → bradycardia + hypotension

item.name: STAGE 5 — Theme Clustering
item.description: POST /retrieval/cluster — HDBSCAN + UMAP across all stored incidents. Uses min_cluster_size=2 for small datasets.

item.name: S5-1 Cluster (min_size=2, no LLM naming)

request.description:
Runs HDBSCAN + UMAP over all 10 incidents.

min_cluster_size=2 (lower than the default 3) because the dataset is small.

Expected clusters (approximate):
- Airway/Difficult intubation: Incidents 1, 5, 9
- Bronchospasm/Respiratory: Incidents 3, 4
- Regional block complications: Incidents 6, 7
- Others may be noise

If n_clusters=0: use summary_report field for pattern text instead.

Response fields:
- total_incidents, n_clusters, noise_count, noise_ratio
- themes[]: theme_id, theme_name, incident_count, patterns, key_insight, recommendations
- umap_coords[]: x, y, incident_id (for visualisation)
- summary_report: full text narrative

item.name: S5-2 Cluster (min_size=2, LLM naming — needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)

exec: pm.test('n_clusters > 0 (LLM naming requires clusters to exist)', function() {

request.description:
Same as S5-1 but Claude names each theme.

min_cluster_size MUST be 2 for a 10-incident dataset — using 3 causes HDBSCAN to classify every incident as noise and LLM naming never runs.

Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env — adds ~1-2s per cluster.

Theme names will be clinical (e.g. 'Difficult Airway Management in High-BMI Patients') instead of generic 'theme_000'.

Note confirmed in live testing (June 13, 2026): min_cluster_size=3 on this dataset → n_clusters=0, noise_count=10. Fixed to min_cluster_size=2.

item.name: STAGE 6 — Grounded RAG (Week 8)
item.description: POST /retrieval/rag/grounded — intent detection + keyword expansion + evidence grading + citations. All 5 intent variants pre-filled.

item.name: S6-1 Grounded RAG: General Intent (airway complications)

request.description:
General intent (no root-cause/pattern/safety trigger words).

Expect:
- intent: 'general'
- keywords: ['airway', 'complications', 'anaesthesia', 'induction', 'operating', 'room']
- expanded_terms: includes 'anesthesia', 'anaesthetic'
- suggested_filters: null
- confidence: 'High' or 'Moderate'
- citations: one line per result e.g.
  'Incident abc123 | severity=... | type=... | score=0.891'
- grounded_context: full evidence block with [HIGH]/[MODERATE]/[LOW] grade labels

item.name: S6-2 Grounded RAG: Root Cause Intent

request.description:
Trigger word 'why' → intent='root_cause'

Expect:
- intent: 'root_cause'
- keywords: includes 'bronchospasm', 'laparoscopic', 'surgery', 'anaesthesia'
- expanded_terms: includes 'anesthesia'
- suggested_filters: null (no severity/year in query)

Top matches:
- Incident 4: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy, peritoneal insufflation trigger
- Incident 3: Gynaecology DHC, bronchospasm + equipment failure

item.name: S6-3 Grounded RAG: Pattern Analysis Intent

request.description:
Trigger phrase 'recurring patterns' → intent='pattern_analysis'

Expect:
- intent: 'pattern_analysis'
- keywords: includes 'difficult', 'intubation', 'emergency', 'surgery'

Top matches:
- Incident 1: Emergency Vascular/Ortho, CL IIb → bougie
- Incident 5: Emergency Laparotomy, RSI COPD patient
- Incident 9: Elective Bariatric, anticipated difficult airway

item.name: S6-4 Grounded RAG: Safety Recommendations Intent

request.description:
Trigger words 'prevent' + 'safety' → intent='safety_recommendations'

Expect:
- intent: 'safety_recommendations'
- keywords: includes 'spinal', 'regional', 'anaesthesia', 'safety'
- expanded_terms: includes 'anesthesia', 'anaesthetic'

Top matches:
- Incident 7: Failed spinal ACL reconstruction, L3-L4 bupivacaine, conversion to GA
- Incident 6: Brainstem anaesthesia after peribulbar block

item.name: S6-5 Grounded RAG: Similar Incidents Intent

request.description:
Trigger phrase 'similar to' → intent='similar_incidents'

Expect:
- intent: 'similar_incidents'
- keywords: includes 'morbidly', 'obese', 'anticipated', 'difficult', 'airway'

Top match:
- Incident 9: 41-50 Female, BMI 43, Bariatric sleeve gastrectomy, video laryngoscopy, outcome D1

item.name: S6-6 Grounded RAG: Preprocessing Bypassed

request.description:
use_preprocessing=false — skips intent detection, keyword extraction, filter inference.

Expect:
- suggested_filters: null (even though 'difficult' appears in query)
- coverage_score: 1.0 (no keywords = full coverage by default)
- Still returns semantically relevant results via pure vector similarity

Compare results with S6-1 to see the difference preprocessing makes.

item.name: STAGE 7 — Incident API (Parse + Analyze)
item.description: Endpoints under /incidents/ — these parse/analyze the Excel without touching the vector store. File upload for parse and analyze, no embedding involved.

item.name: S7-1 Parse Excel → Incident JSON (file upload)

request.description:
Parses Excel → returns array of structured Incident objects (no embedding, no Qdrant).

Save the full response — you need it for S7-3 (AI Analysis with JSON input).

Expected response: array of 10 Incident objects with fields:
- incident_id, patient.age_range, patient.sex
- surgery.surgical_branch, surgery.type_of_procedure
- incident.incident_description, incident.incident_type[]
- anesthesia.primary_technique
- outcome.harm_severity, outcome.outcome_category
- metadata.source_file

item.name: S7-2 Analyze + Parse Excel in One Shot (file upload, needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)

request.description:
Parses Excel + runs AI analysis on each incident in one call.

Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env — takes ~30-60s for 10 incidents.

Returns array of AIAnalysis objects with:
- incident_id, root_cause, key_learning
- contributing_factors[], preventability_score
- severity (AI-assessed, may differ from raw Excel)
- recommendations[]

Save the response — use it with S7-3 for the rich embedding path.

item.name: S7-3 List Stored Incidents (persistence mode only)
STAGE 8 — Rich Metadata Path (AI Analysis + Embedding)
item.description: 3-step workflow to embed incidents WITH AI-analysed metadata (root_cause, key_learning, AI-assessed severity). Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Steps must run in order: S8-1 → S8-2 → S8-3.

item.name: S8-1 Step 1: Parse Excel → Save Response (file upload)

exec: console.log('Saved ' + json.length + ' incidents to collection variable incidents_json');

request.description:
Step 1 of 3 in the rich metadata workflow.

Parse Excel → get Incident JSON array.

The test script auto-saves the response to {{incidents_json}} collection variable so Step 2 can use it.

item.name: S8-2 Step 2: AI Analyze Incidents → Save Response (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)

exec: console.log('Saved ' + json.count + ' analyses to collection variable analyses_json');

body.raw: {{incidents_json}}

request.description:
Step 2 of 3 — uses {{incidents_json}} set automatically by S8-1.

Runs Claude AI analysis on each incident (~30-60s for 10 incidents).

Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.

Returns: { count: 10, analyses: [...AIAnalysis objects...] }

The test script auto-saves the analyses array to {{analyses_json}} for Step 3.

item.name: S8-3 Step 3: Ingest with Rich AI Metadata

request.description:
Step 3 of 3 — uses {{incidents_json}} and {{analyses_json}} set by S8-1 and S8-2.

Embeds incidents paired with AI analysis — resulting vectors carry rich metadata:
- root_cause (Claude-generated)
- key_learning (Claude-generated)
- severity (AI-assessed, more accurate than raw Excel)
- contributing_factors

After this, all search/RAG/cluster endpoints return populated root_cause and key_learning fields instead of empty strings.
